name: Marinated Green Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon crushed small dried hot chile peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Snip the ends from the beans and wash them thoroughly. Leave them whole or cut them in half if they are long.
2. Bring 2 quarts of water to a boil in a large pan. Add 3 tablespoons of salt and the beans. Return to a boil, then cook the beans uncovered for about 5 minutes or until they are just tender crisp. Drain immediately and cool.
3. Pack the cooled beans into four refrigerator containers (1 pint each).
4. In each container, sprinkle half a teaspoon of mustard seed, dill weed, one-quarter teaspoon of chilies, and one-quarter teaspoon of dill seed.
5. Add one clove of garlic to each container.
6. Bring 2 cups of water, vinegar, and the remaining 3 tablespoons of salt to a boil in a small saucepan. Pour the boiling mixture over the beans in the containers.
7. Cover and chill overnight or for up to 2 weeks.
Instead of green beans, you can use about 2 pounds of carrots or cauliflower, or a mixture of the three vegetables. Peel the carrots and cut them into thin sticks. Separate the cauliflower into florets; reduce cooking time to 3 minutes or until tender.
